FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class StringTable

Namespace
FluentGpu.Foundation
Assembly
FluentGpu.Foundation.dll

String interner with REF-COUNTED reclamation. Lives in Foundation so Render can resolve without depending on Scene.

 <p><b>Seam-safe (threading-render-seam.md §9):</b> the UI thread interns (<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Intern(System.String)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>, the single writer) while
 the render thread resolves (<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Resolve(FluentGpu.Foundation.StringId)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>) the previous frame's ids concurrently. The backing store is therefore
 <i>chunked</i>: chunks are allocated once and never resized or moved, so a concurrent reader can never observe a torn or
 reallocated array (a plain <code>List<string></code> would race on its geometric Add-realloc). A release-store of the count
 publishes each new slot; the reader acquire-loads the count before indexing, so it only ever reads fully-written slots.</p>

 <p><b>Reclamation (virtualization workloads):</b> a 100k-row virtual list streams unique row text through the table —
 append-only interning is an unbounded leak. Owners (the scene's text columns) pair <xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.AddRef(FluentGpu.Foundation.StringId)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>/<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Release(FluentGpu.Foundation.StringId)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>;
 when an id's count hits 0 its map entry is removed (a re-intern of the same content gets a NEW id) and the slot is cleared
 after a reader quarantine (<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Tick" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> per frame, far beyond the in-flight frame depth). <b>Ids are never reused</b> —
 reclaimed ids stay burned, so a stale id can never alias different content (the shaped-run cache keys on ids and ages out in
 well under the id space). Fully-dead chunks free their backing array. Strings never AddRef'd are simply permanent.</p>

public sealed class StringTable

Constructors

StringTable() 

public StringTable()

Properties

IdHighWater

The monotonic next-id counter (one past the highest minted id) — ids never reuse, so this only grows.

public int IdHighWater { get; }

Property Value

int

MapCount

Live map entries (interned strings currently resolvable by content) — diagnostics/leak checks.

public int MapCount { get; }

Property Value

int

PendingReclaim

Releases whose slots await the reader quarantine — diagnostics.

public int PendingReclaim { get; }

Property Value

int

PendingReclaimCount

Releases whose slots await the reader quarantine (census alias of PendingReclaim).

public int PendingReclaimCount { get; }

Property Value

int

Methods

AddRef(StringId)

Take an ownership reference on an id (UI thread). Owners are the scene's text columns; pair with Release(StringId).

public void AddRef(StringId id)

Parameters

id StringId

Intern(string?)

Intern a string to a stable id. UI-thread only (the single writer). A live id is never moved; reclaimed ids are never reused (re-interning previously-released content yields a fresh id).

public StringId Intern(string? s)

Parameters

s string

Returns

StringId

Release(StringId)

Drop an ownership reference (UI thread). The LAST release removes the map entry and schedules the slot clear behind the reader quarantine. Releasing a never-AddRef'd (permanent) id is a no-op.

public void Release(StringId id)

Parameters

id StringId

Resolve(StringId)

Resolve an id to its string. Safe to call from the render thread concurrently with Intern(string?).

public string Resolve(StringId id)

Parameters

id StringId

Returns

string

Tick()

Advance the reclamation clock one frame (host, once per painted frame): clears slots whose quarantine elapsed and frees fully-dead chunks. UI thread.

public void Tick()
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
